title: Engira

{{Quote|…replenishment convoy ambushed outside of Engira, resulting in loss of three freighters carrying several hundred metric tons of food and ammunition valued at 18.2 million standard credits…|Excerpt from the Imperial Commission on the Conduct of the War's report "Effectiveness of Standard Escort and Patrol Tactics Against Enemy Interdiction"|<ref name="SWS2">[[The Star Wars Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Star Wars Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref>}}
'''Engira''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] located in the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]]. The site of a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] base that was destroyed more than thirty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] had become a supply [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. An ambush near Engira once led to the loss of three Imperial [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] transporting [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] and ammunition.

==Description==
Engira was a [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]–type [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="SWR" /> located in the [[Engira system/Legends|Engira system]], a part of the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]] within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Slice" /> It had [[Cloud/Legends|clouds]] that formed bands of [[Color/Legends|red]], orange, and white coloration.<ref name="SWR" />

==History==
At some point between [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]] and [[34 BBY/Legends|34 BBY]], [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]] forces under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of the [[Harch/Legends|Harch]] military leader [[Trench/Legends|Trench]] destroyed a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] base at Engira.<ref name="EGTW" /> After the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Engira came to serve as an Imperial supply [[Colonist/Legends|colony]], from which [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] transported supplies to Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]] and other facilities throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="SWR" />

As a result, Engira became a prime target for pirates,<ref name="SWR" /> and during the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], forces affiliated with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] ambushed a replenishment convoy near Engira. The loss of the convoy's three [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], which had been carrying several hundred [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]] and ammunition valued at 18.2 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], was among several incidents later mentioned by the [[Imperial Commission on the Conduct of the War]] in the report "[[Effectiveness of Standard Escort and Patrol Tactics Against Enemy Interdiction]],"<ref name="SWS2" /> with the commission recommending that the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] adopt the usage of [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|escort frigates]] for the protection of the Empire's convoys.<ref name="CSWE Engira">{{CSWECite|I|237|Engira}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Starwarssourcebook1st.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Engira was introduced in ''The Star Wars Sourcebook''.]]
Engira was first mentioned in ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'', a [[1987]] supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[Curtis Smith]].<ref name="SWS">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed Engira in the [[Mahrusha sector/Legends|Mahrusha sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process, its [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity was never confirmed.<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref>

The planet's first full appearance came in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which instead placed Engira in the Jospro [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and also provided the planet's only visual depiction—an image also used to illustrate three other worlds in the game.<ref name="SWR" /> The game's accompanying [[Prima Games]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] misspelled Engira's name as "Enigra."<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Engira [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Engira itself, in grid square R-7,<ref name="TEA" /> and the later [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book ultimately confirmed ''Star Wars: Rebellion''{{'s}} placement of the system and the planet.<ref name="Slice" />
